title: Classic Hummus Tahini Olive Oil

seo.title: How to Make Classic Hummus Tahini Olive Oil — Easy Middle Eastern Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Classic Hummus Tahini Olive Oil. This easy Middle Eastern recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.

easy Classic Hummus Tahini Olive Oil recipe
authentic Middle Eastern Classic Hummus Tahini Olive Oil

Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: Authentic Middle Eastern Classic Hummus Tahini Olive Oil recipe
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade Classic Hummus Tahini Olive Oil — a beloved Middle Eastern classic

description: Classic Hummus Tahini Olive Oil is a creamy, rich dip, a staple of Middle Eastern cuisine. Made from wholesome chickpeas, nutty tahini, bright lemon, and aromatic garlic, it's a versatile appetizer perfect for any gathering. Drizzled generously with extra virgin olive oil, it offers a delightful blend of earthy and zesty flavors, ready to be scooped up with warm pita bread or fresh vegetables.

instructions.title: Prepare Chickpeas
instructions.text: Drain and rinse the canned chickpeas thoroughly under cold water. For an ultra-smooth hummus, you can optionally remove the skins from the chickpeas by gently rubbing them between your fingers. This step requires a bit of patience but yields a superior texture. Set aside.

instructions.title: Combine Main Ingredients
instructions.text: In the bowl of a food processor, combine the drained chickpeas, tahini, freshly squeezed lemon juice, minced garlic, salt, and ground cumin (if using).

instructions.title: Process Until Smooth
instructions.text: Process the mixture, scraping down the sides of the bowl as needed. While the processor is running, slowly add the cold water (or reserved chickpea liquid) through the feed tube, a tablespoon at a time, until the hummus reaches your desired creamy and smooth consistency. This usually takes about 3-5 minutes of continuous processing.

instructions.title: Adjust Seasoning
instructions.text: Stop the food processor and taste the hummus. Adjust seasonings as necessary, adding more salt, lemon juice, or tahini to achieve your preferred balance of flavors. If you prefer a lighter, airier texture, you can add a touch more water and process briefly again.

instructions.title: Transfer and Chill
instructions.text: Transfer the prepared hummus to a serving bowl. Cover it tightly with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 15-20 minutes. Chilling allows the flavors to meld beautifully and enhances the overall taste.

instructions.title: Garnish and Serve
instructions.text: Before serving, remove the hummus from the refrigerator. Create a shallow well or swirl on the surface of the hummus using the back of a spoon. Drizzle generously with extra virgin olive oil and sprinkle with paprika or sumac for color and a subtle tangy kick. Serve immediately with warm pita bread, fresh vegetable sticks, or as part of a mezze platter.

tips: For the smoothest hummus, remove the skins from the chickpeas after draining. It's a bit time-consuming but makes a noticeable difference in texture.
Always use good quality tahini and freshly squeezed lemon juice. The quality of these key ingredients significantly impacts the final flavor of your hummus.
If your hummus is too thick, add a tablespoon of ice water or reserved chickpea liquid at a time while processing until it reaches the perfect creamy consistency.
article: Classic Hummus Tahini Olive Oil is a beloved Middle Eastern Appetizers dish enjoyed across Middle Eastern. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
